commit.message:
eng: remove debug console output for production cleanliness

Remove console.log/warn from templateResolver (fires on every config lookup),
writeBrainFact audit stub, useSubscription provisioning logs,
AdminTestOnboardingPage industry load log, createDefaultWorkflows success log.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(auth): 429 rate limit backoff + admin_settings RLS simplification

1. Supabase client now uses custom fetch with automatic retry + exponential
   backoff for 429 responses. Prevents the auth death spiral where rate-limited
   token refresh fires SIGNED_OUT → clears session → retry → 429 again.

2. Simplified admin_settings RLS from has_role() dependency to simple
   user_id = auth.uid(). The has_role() check created a circular dependency
   with user_roles RLS, causing 42501 on INSERT for super_admin users.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

eng: fix admin_settings RLS INSERT policy (42501)

Split single FOR ALL policy into separate SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE
policies. The combined policy was causing 42501 insufficient_privilege
on INSERT for super_admin users. Migration deployed via API.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- app/patterns/layout.tsx — replaced inline nav with <SiteNav />
- components/Nav.tsx — scoped to Groundtrust sub-nav only: How It Works, For Stewards, For Funders, For Land Trusts (removed About)

refactor: switch to official drizzle-kit migration workflow

Replace hand-written SQL files + custom runner with drizzle-kit's
official generate/migrate system.

Workflow from now on:
  1. Change schema.ts
  2. npx drizzle-kit generate
  3. Commit generated SQL + snapshot + journal
  4. Deploy — migrate() applies pending migrations automatically

Deleted 6 manual SQL files, replaced with single baseline generated
by drizzle-kit. Production DB seeded with __drizzle_migrations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Auto-infer connection aggregates from node type fields

Aggregates are now automatically inferred from the node type's field
definitions instead of requiring an explicit `aggregate do ... end`
block. Numeric fields (integer, float, decimal) get sum/avg/min/max,
temporal fields (date, datetime, etc.) get min/max only. Fields can
opt out with `aggregate: false`.

- Add `infer_aggregates/1` to ConnectionAggregate
- Use original field types for min/max (not hardcoded :string)
- Wire inference into both type.ex and connection.ex paths
- Add total_count, exists, nodes, aggregate fields to type.ex connections
- Strip `aggregate:` option from field opts before Absinthe
- Remove explicit aggregate DSL macros (aggregate/sum/avg/min/max)

commit.message:
fix: update (README.md)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: TTS prefetch consumed + STT gate auto-restart loop

#9 — TTS prefetch wasted: _prefetchedBlob was stored but never used in the
next _playNext() iteration. Now checks _prefetchedBlob first via ?? before
calling _fetchAudio(), eliminating redundant TTS API calls. Also cleared
on stop() and queue empty to prevent stale blobs.

#10 — STT gate infinite loop: onend auto-restart only checked gateEnabled
&& !gatePaused, causing infinite start/end/start cycles during silence.
Added gateOpen check so recognition only restarts when sound is actually
above threshold. _monitorGate will re-trigger when new sound is detected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: update (README.md)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: replace E prehook Check 6 inline script with credential-health-check.mjs (R#309)

Check 6 had a 50-line inline Node script duplicating credential validation
logic already provided by credential-health-check.mjs. Now delegates to the
module, gaining JWT expiry detection and consolidating credential logic in
one place. E prehook reduced from 525 to 490 lines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reestructuración completa del proyecto: 4 casos de estudio

- Renombrar PublicationE → CaseStudy_4 (Oscilador de Duffing)
- Crear CaseStudy_1: y' + y² = sin(5t)
- Crear CaseStudy_2: y' + sin²(y) = sin(5t)
- Crear CaseStudy_3: y' + β(y) con RBF

Cambios principales:
- Eliminar carpetas PublicationA-D (código antiguo)
- Implementación modular y autocontenida por caso
- README.md principal actualizado con documentación completa
- Agregar requirements.txt con dependencias
- Limpiar archivos redundantes (notebooks, PDFs, etc.)

Cada caso ahora incluye:
- Módulo principal con regresor homotópico
- Suite de tests y análisis de convergencia
- Script de generación de figuras
- README.md con documentación detallada

config dropdown. 11 new tests, 100% use-case coverage.

feat(auth): add user registration, BCrypt auth, and per-user stats isolation

- Replace hardcoded demo credentials with real DB-backed auth using BCrypt
  (BCryptPasswordEncoder, UserRepository, V6 Flyway migration sets demo hash)
- Add POST /v1/auth/register endpoint; returns 201 + JWT on success,
  409 on duplicate email; email normalised to lowercase before storage
- Add AuthResponse and RegisterRequest DTOs; constructor-inject jwtSecret
- Add displayName column to users table (V6__add_auth_columns.sql)
- Fix stats-service to extract userId from JWT instead of hardcoding
  DEMO_USER_ID; cache keys are now per-user to prevent data leakage
- Add Redis to stats-listener; evict affected user's cache keys immediately
  after each Kafka event so dashboard reflects changes without waiting for TTL
- Add Register.tsx page with client-side password-match validation and
  structured error surfacing; add /register public route to router
- Update AuthContext with signUp(), shared handleAuthResponse(), and
  displayName support; fix Login.tsx error extraction
- Update screenshots and README: register page, auth API docs, features

feat: implement customer management API endpoints

backend API endpoints were never implemented. The frontend calls

route /api/v1/customers/* to the admin service Lambda.

commit.message: fix: stop TipTap from forcing bold on every keystroke The useEffect was calling setContent() whenever the editor's getHTML() output differed from the value prop — which happened after every keystroke because TipTap normalizes HTML slightly. setContent() resets the cursor to position 0 (inside Bio), so the next character typed inherited the bold mark. Each keystroke reset the cursor, locking bold on. Fix: track the last HTML emitted by onUpdate in a ref. Only call setContent when value changes to something the editor didn't produce itself (i.e. a genuinely external change, like switching to a different entity). Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6

implement wlst_connect.sh – interactive WLST shell with auto-login

Loads credentials from weblogic_sec.conf.des3 via load_weblogic_password().
Password is passed to WLST via env vars (not stored in the bootstrap .py file).
Uses WLST interact() after connect() to stay in interactive mode.
EXIT trap deletes temp script and clears password from environment.

Supports --url and --user overrides; sources setDomainEnv.sh when available.

commit.message:
fix: WebKit 2.44.0-2 pinnen – EGL_BAD_PARAMETER auf KDE Plasma/Wayland behoben

Neuere WebKit-Versionen (2.46+) verursachen beim Start einen
EGL_BAD_PARAMETER-Crash auf bestimmten Linux-Desktops (Bazzite, Fedora KDE
Plasma u.a.) – bekannter Tauri-Bug (tauri-apps/tauri#11994).

Lösung (analog OmniKee/audioling-Issue #40):
- build.yml: ubuntu-latest → ubuntu-24.04
- build.yml: libwebkit2gtk-4.1 + libjavascriptcoregtk-4.1 auf 2.44.0-2 gepinnt
- lib.rs: Env-Var-Workaround entfernt (Root-Fix im Build-System)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Align 4 footprints to JLCPCB EasyEDA library, add polarity verification

JLCPCB flagged J1 (USB-C), J4 (FPC 40-pin), SW_PWR (slide switch),
and U6 (SD card) as package mismatch. Fetched exact pad dimensions
from EasyEDA API and aligned all 4 footprints to match.

Footprint changes:
- J1: pads 0.55x1.1mm, pins renamed 1-14, shield drill 0.6mm, +2 NPTH
- J4: pads 0.3x1.5mm, mounting 2.0x2.5mm at Y=+1.288, pins 41/42
- SW_PWR: pads 0.6x1.524mm, asymmetric shell Y, +2 NPTH holes
- U6: signal Y→-5.276mm, shield Y corrected, +2 NPTH holes

Also updated: routing.py (J1 pin names), jlcpcb_export.py (zeroed
position corrections), verify_dfm_v2.py (baselines), Makefile.

New: verify_polarity.py — 38 tests, 208 pin-to-net checks covering
all 64 components. Integrated in make verify-all.

Result: 64 DFM + 9 DFA + 38 polarity = 111 tests, 0 failures.
